title: Rubus lepidulus
intro: Rubus lepidulus, also known as the hairy dewberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against cell damage, reduce inflammation, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Rubus lepidulus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Rubus lepidulus is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and jams, with its berries often used in traditional herbal medicine to promote digestive health and reduce oxidative stress.
taxonomy: Rubus lepidulus is a species of flowering plant in the genus Rubus, which is part of the family Rosaceae. This genus includes many types of brambles and raspberries. Rubus lepidulus is a shrub native to parts of Asia and Europe. It is classified as a species within the section Rubus and the subsection Rubini. The plant's classification is further detailed within the broader context of the subgenus Idaeobatus and the tribe Rubieae.
